title: Rooted Trust
story:
A gardener planted two trees in different places. One was planted near a flowing stream, and the other was planted far away on dry ground.

At first, both trees looked the same. They were small, green, and full of promise.

But as time passed, the seasons changed. The sun grew hotter, and the rains became less frequent.

The tree near the stream continued to grow strong. Its leaves stayed green. Its roots reached deep into the water source.

The other tree began to struggle. Its leaves dried. Its growth slowed. It depended only on the rain that did not always come.

In life, we often face seasons of heat and difficulty.

But when our trust is rooted in God, we are sustained from within.

Even when circumstances are hard, we remain steady, nourished, and full of life.

Like the tree by the water, our strength comes from a source that never runs dry.
life_lesson:
When your trust is rooted in God, you will remain steady in every season.

True strength comes from depending on Him, not on changing circumstances.
prayer:
Dear Heavenly Father,

Help me to place my full trust in You.

Let my life be like a tree planted by the water.

When difficult seasons come, keep me strong and steady.

Teach me to depend on You as my constant source.

Remove fear and fill my heart with confidence in Your care.

In Jesus' name,
Amen.
reflection_question:
Where have you been placing your trust recently?

Are you depending on circumstances or on God as your source?

How can you deepen your trust in Him today?
verse:
Blessed is the man who trusts in the Lord, whose confidence is in Him.
He will be like a tree planted by the water that sends out its roots by the stream.
It does not fear when heat comes; its leaves are always green.
